name: tesscube
documentation:
# tesscube

```{image} https://github.com/tessgi/tesscube/actions/workflows/python-app.yml/badge.svg
:alt: Test status
:target: https://github.com/tessgi/tesscube/actions/workflows/python-app.yml
```

```{image} https://badge.fury.io/py/tesscube.svg
:alt: PyPI version
:target: https://badge.fury.io/py/tesscube
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/badge/documentation-live-blue.svg
:alt: Documentation
:target: https://tessgi.github.io/tesscube/
```

% <!-- intro content start -->

`tesscube` is a package designed to help you obtain TESS data by cutting it out of the FFI cubes at the [Barbara A. Mikulski Archive for Space Telescopes (MAST)](https://mast.stsci.edu/portal/Mashup/Clients/Mast/Portal.html).

`tesscube` works with data that is available in the cloud, and will return TESS data in formats similar to the NASA TESS official mission products. You do not need any login credentials to use `tesscube`, and can use this tool by pip installing either on your local machine or in the cloud.

:::{note}
Data formats
`tesscube` will return `astropy` native objects, including `astropy.fits.HDUList` objects, and `astropy.wcs.WCS` objects. You can work with these directly, or load them into `lightkurve`.
:::

% <!-- intro content end -->

% <!-- quickstart content start -->

## Quickstart

The easiest way to install `tesscube` and all of its dependencies is to use the `pip` command.

To install `tesscube`, run the following command in a terminal window:

```console
$ python -m pip install tesscube --upgrade
```

The `--upgrade` flag is optional, but recommended if you already
have `tesscube` installed and want to upgrade to the latest version.

Depending on the specific Python environment, you may need to replace `python`
with the correct Python interpreter, e.g., `python3`.

### Load an FFI cube

You can work with an FFI cube by loading it using a sector, camera, and CCD number.

```python
from tesscube import TESSCube
cube = TESSCube(sector=1, camera=1, ccd=4)
```

### Obtain an FFI Using `tesscube`

You can obtain an FFI image by indexing into a cube

```python
from tesscube import TESSCube
cube = TESSCube(sector=1, camera=1, ccd=4)
ffi = cube[300]
```

This will return an `astropy.fits.HDUList`

### Obtain a TPF

You can obtain a TPF in two ways, either you can either pass a pixel position

```python
from tesscube import TESSCube
from astropy.coordinates import SkyCoord
corner = (1282, 1750)
cube = TESSCube(sector=1, camera=1, ccd=4)
tpf = cube.get_tpf(corner, shape=(10, 11))
```

Or you can pass an astropy SkyCoord object containing the RA and Dec of the target

```python
from tesscube import TESSCube
from astropy.coordinates import SkyCoord
coord = SkyCoord.from_name("AU Mic")
cube = TESSCube(sector=1, camera=1, ccd=4)
tpf = cube.get_tpf(coord, shape=(10, 11))
```

Alternatively, you can index into the cube like so:

```python
from tesscube import TESSCube
cube = TESSCube(sector=1, camera=1, ccd=4)
tpf = cube[:, 401:410, 503:510]
```

Both will return an `astropy.fits.HDUList`, with a file format similar to the official mission products.

### Obtain a lower time resolution TPF

You can obtain a lower time resolution by either passing in a `frame_bin` parameter, which will downsample the resultant TPF,

```python
from tesscube import TESSCube
from astropy.coordinates import SkyCoord
corner = (1282, 1750)
cube = TESSCube(sector=1, camera=1, ccd=4)
tpf = cube.get_tpf(corner, shape=(10, 11), frame_bin=10)
```

Or you can slice the cube, which will return a downsampled TPF

```python
from tesscube import TESSCube
cube = TESSCube(sector=1, camera=1, ccd=4)
tpf = cube[::10, 401:410, 503:510]
```

Both will return an `astropy.fits.HDUList`, with a file format similar to the official mission products, with the time resolution reduced by a factor of 10.

% <!-- quickstart content end -->

% <!-- Contributing content start -->

## Contributing

`tesscube` is an open-source, community driven package.
We welcome users to contribute and develop new features for lksearch.

For further information, please see the [Lightkurve Community guidelines](https://docs.lightkurve.org/development/contributing.html).

% <!-- Contributing content end -->

% <!-- Citing content start -->

## Citing

If you find `tesscube` useful in your research, please cite it and give us a GitHub star!

If you use Lightkurve for work or research presented in a publication, we request the following acknowledgment or citation:

`This research made use of Lightkurve, a Python package for Kepler and TESS data analysis (Lightkurve Collaboration, 2018).`

See full citation instuctions, including dependencies, in the [Lightkurve documentation](https://docs.lightkurve.org/about/citing.html).

% <!-- Citing content end -->

% <!-- Contact content start -->

## Contact

`tesscube` is an open source community project created by the [TESS Science Support Center].
The best way to contact us is to [open an issue] or to e-mail <mailto:tesshelp@bigbang.gsfc.nasa.gov>.

Please include a self-contained example that fully demonstrates your problem or question.
:

% <!-- Contact content end -->

% <!-- Changelog content start -->

documentation: Utilities to help work with cube data

functions.name: tesscube.utils.convert_to_native_types
documentation:
Recursively convert objects in a data structure to native Python types.
Handles dictionaries, lists, and NumPy data types.

functions.name: tesscube.utils.convert_coordinates_to_runs
documentation: Converts a list of (row, column) coordinates to a list of (start_row, end_row, column) coordinates.

functions.name: tesscube.get_logger
documentation: Configure and return a logger with RichHandler.

documentation: Make sure that we have a directory we can put package config in

documentation: bytes

variables.name: tesscube.BYTES_PER_PIX

modules.name: tesscube.query
documentation: Mixin class to enable queries. This is in a separate class for readability.

functions.name: tesscube.query.async_get_primary_hdu
documentation:
Asynchronously retrieves the primary HDU of a cube FITS file.

Parameters
----------
object_key : str
    The S3 object key of the FITS file (i.e. the file name)

Returns
-------
astropy.io.fits.PrimaryHDU
    The primary HDU of the FITS file, with corrected headers.

functions.name: tesscube.query.async_get_last_hdu
documentation:
Asynchronously retrieves the last HDU of a cube FITS file.

Parameters
----------
object_key : str
    The S3 object key of the FITS file  (i.e. the file name)
end : int
    The byte position to start retrieving data from.
Returns
-------
astropy.io.fits.PrimaryHDU
    The last HDU of the FITS file.

functions.name: tesscube.query.async_get_ffi
documentation:
Asynchronously retrieves a full-frame image (FFI) FITS file.

Parameters
----------
ffi_name : str
    The name of the full-frame image (FFI) as stored in s3.

Returns
-------
astropy.io.fits.HDUList
    The HDU list of the retrieved FFI FITS file.

methods.name: tesscube.query.QueryMixin.find_byte_offset
documentation:
Returns the byte offset of a specific pixel position.

Parameters
----------
row : int
    The row index of the pixel (1-indexed).
column : int
    The column index of the pixel (1-indexed).
frame : int, optional
    The frame index, by default 0.

Returns
-------
offset: int
    The byte offset of the specified pixel.

methods.name: tesscube.query.QueryMixin.find_byte_range_subframes
documentation:
Returns the byte range for a given row, column, and frame range.

Parameters
----------
row : int
    The row index of the pixel (1-indexed).
column : int
    The column index of the pixel (1-indexed).
frame_range : tuple of int, optional
    The range of frames to consider, by default None.

Returns
-------
tuple of int
    The byte range (start, end) for the specified parameters.

methods.name: tesscube.query.QueryMixin.find_byte_range
documentation:
Returns the byte range for a given row and column range.

Parameters
----------
row : int
    The row index of the pixel (1-indexed).
column_range : tuple of int
    The range of columns to consider.

Returns
-------
tuple of int
    The byte range (start, end) for the specified parameters.

methods.name: tesscube.query.QueryMixin.async_get_data_per_pixel
documentation:
Asynchronously retrieves data for a specific pixel over a range of frames.

Parameters
----------
s3 : aiobotocore.client.AioBaseClient
    The S3 client used to fetch the data.
semaphore : asyncio.Semaphore
    The semaphore to limit the number of concurrent downloads.
column : int
    The column index of the pixel (1-indexed).
row : int
    The row index of the pixel (1-indexed).
frame_range : tuple of int, optional
    The range of frames to consider, by default None.

Returns
-------
data: np.ndarray
    The pixel values for the specified range of frames.

methods.name: tesscube.query.QueryMixin.async_get_data_per_row
documentation:
Asynchronously retrieves data for a specific row over a range of columns.

Parameters
----------
s3 : aiobotocore.client.AioBaseClient
    The S3 client used to fetch the data.
semaphore : asyncio.Semaphore
    The semaphore to limit the number of concurrent downloads.
start_column : int
    The starting column index (1-indexed).
ncolumns : int
    The number of columns to retrieve.
row : int, optional
    The row index of the pixel (1-indexed), by default 1.

Returns
-------
data: np.ndarray
    The pixel values for the specified row and columns.

methods.name: tesscube.query.QueryMixin.async_get_data_per_rows
documentation:
Asynchronously retrieves data for multiple rows.

Parameters
----------
runs : list of tuples
    A list of tuples containing parameters for each run.

Returns
-------
data: np.ndarray
    The pixel values for the specified rows.

methods.name: tesscube.query.QueryMixin.async_get_data_per_block
documentation:
Asynchronously retrieves data for a block of pixels.

Parameters
----------
start_column : int
    The starting column index (1-indexed).
ncolumns : int
    The number of columns to retrieve.
start_row : int
    The starting row index (1-indexed).
nrows : int
    The number of rows to retrieve.
frame_range : tuple of int, optional
    The range of frames to consider, by default None.

Returns
-------
data: numpy.ndarray
    The pixel values for the specified block.

methods.name: tesscube.query.QueryMixin.get_pixel_timeseries
documentation:
Retrieves the pixel time series for the specified coordinates.

Parameters
----------
coordinates : tuple or list of tuple
    The coordinates (row, column) to retrieve time series for.
return_errors : bool, optional
    Whether to return the errors alongside the flux, by default False.

Returns
-------
data: numpy.ndarray or tuple of numpy.ndarray
    The flux (and optionally errors) time series for the specified coordinates.

methods.name: tesscube.query.QueryMixin.async_get_flux
documentation:
Asynchronously retrieves flux data for a block of pixels.

Parameters
----------
start_column : int
    The starting column index (1-indexed).
ncolumns : int
    The number of columns to retrieve.
start_row : int
    The starting row index (1-indexed).
nrows : int
    The number of rows to retrieve.
frame_range : tuple of int, optional
    The range of frames to consider, by default None.

Returns
-------
tuple of numpy.ndarray
    The flux and flux error for the specified block.

methods.name: tesscube.query.QueryMixin.get_flux
documentation:
Retrieves the flux and flux error for a specific block of pixels.

This method caches the result to avoid redundant calculations. The flux
and flux error are retrieved for a rectangular block of pixels specified
by the `corner` and `shape` parameters. The data can also be restricted
to a specific range of frames using the `frame_range` parameter.

Parameters
----------
corner : tuple of int, optional
    The (row, column) coordinates of the top-left corner of the pixel block,
    by default (1014, 1058).
shape : tuple of int, optional
    The (number of rows, number of columns) shape of the pixel block,
    by default (20, 21).
frame_range : tuple of int, optional
    The (start, end) frame range to retrieve the data, by default None.

Returns
-------
tuple of numpy.ndarray
    A tuple containing:
    - flux: A 3D numpy array of shape (nframes, nrows, ncolumns)
    containing the flux data.
    - flux_err: A 3D numpy array of shape (nframes, nrows, ncolumns)
    containing the flux error data.

modules.name: tesscube.fits
documentation: Functions to fix fits formats

functions.name: tesscube.fits.get_wcs_header_by_extension
documentation: Converts WCS header into the equivalent for each extension

functions.name: tesscube.fits._fix_primary_hdu
documentation:
Fixes the primary HDU header to mimic TESS official products.
This is largely taken from MASTs astrocut package under the BSD license.

functions.name: tesscube.config.set_config_parameter
documentation: Update a configuration parameter

modules.name: tesscube.wcs
documentation: Tools to work with WCS

classes.name: tesscube.wcs.WCSMixin
documentation: Mixins to use the WCS
def _extract_all_WCS(hdu):
"""Extract all the WCSs from a TableHDU from the TESS cube"""
wcss = []
for idx in np.arange(len(hdu.data["CRPIX1"])):
try:
wcs_hdu = fits.PrimaryHDU()
for attr in WCS_ATTRS(hdu):
wcs_hdu.header[attr] = hdu[0].data[attr][idx]
wcs_hdu.header["WCSAXES"] = int(wcs_hdu.header["WCSAXES"])
wcs_hdu.header["WCSAXESP"] = int(wcs_hdu.header["WCSAXESP"])
wcss.append(WCS(wcs_hdu.header))
except:
wcss.append(None)
return wcss

classes.name: tesscube.cube.TESSCube

methods.name: tesscube.cube.TESSCube.header_dict
documentation:
Get the header keywords from the MAST cube.

Returns
-------
dict
    A dictionary of header keywords from the MAST cube.

methods.name: tesscube.cube.TESSCube.from_skycoord
documentation:
Create a TESSCube object from a SkyCoord object.

This method identifies the appropriate TESS cube that contains the
given SkyCoord and returns a TESSCube object.

Parameters
----------
coord : SkyCoord
    The celestial coordinates of the target.
sector : int
    The TESS observation sector number.

Returns
-------
TESSCube
    The TESSCube object containing the specified coordinates.

methods.name: tesscube.cube.TESSCube.primary_hdu
documentation: The primary HDU of the cube file.

methods.name: tesscube.cube.TESSCube.last_hdu
documentation: The last HDU of the cube file.

methods.name: tesscube.cube.TESSCube.ffi_names
documentation: The FFI names used to make the cube.

methods.name: tesscube.cube.TESSCube.get_ffi
documentation:
Retrieve a Full-Frame Image (FFI) from the data cube.

One of `time`, `index`, or `ffi_name` must be provided to identify the FFI.
This method caches up to 4 FFIs for efficient reuse.

Parameters
----------
index : int, optional
    The index of the FFI to retrieve.
time : astropy.time.Time, optional
    The time at which the FFI was captured.
ffi_name : str, optional
    The name of the FFI file.
raw : bool, optional
    Whether to retrieve the raw FFI (default is False).

Returns
-------
astropy.io.fits.HDUList
    The HDUList containing the FFI data.

methods.name: tesscube.cube.TESSCube.get_tpf
documentation:
Retrieve a Target Pixel File (TPF) from the data cube.

The TPF contains the flux data for a specific target region, defined by
either pixel coordinates or a SkyCoord object. The data can be binned
over multiple frames (cadences) using `frame_bin`.

Parameters
----------
target : tuple or SkyCoord, optional
    The (row, column) coordinates or a SkyCoord object for the target region.
shape : tuple of int, optional
    The (number of rows, number of columns) shape of the target region.
frame_range : tuple of int, optional
    The (start, end) frame range to retrieve the data.
frame_bin : int, optional
    The number of frames to bin together (default is 1, i.e. no binning).
calculate_poscorr : bool
    Whether to calculate the `POS_CORR` columns. These are present in SPOC
    data but not present in some HLSPs. This takes extra time to calculate,
    but provides extra position information. Setting to False will result in
    POS_CORR1 and POS_CORR2 being set to zero.

Returns
-------
astropy.io.fits.HDUList
    The HDUList containing the TPF data.

methods.name: tesscube.cube.TESSCube.time
documentation: Time of the frames in BTJD. Note this is the time at the center of the FFI.

methods.name: tesscube.cube.TESSCube.timecorr
documentation: Barycentric time correction for the center of the FFI.

methods.name: tesscube.cube.TESSCube.quality
documentation: SPOC provided quality flags for each cadence.

methods.name: tesscube.cube.TESSCube.cadence_number
documentation: Cadence number for each frame. Note this is not the same as the SPOC provided cadence numbers.

methods.name: tesscube.cube.TESSCube.exposure_time
documentation: Exposure time in days

A class to obtain portions of TESS data cubes from MAST's AWS bucket.

This class provides methods to efficiently retrieve specific portions
of TESS (Transiting Exoplanet Survey Satellite) FFI data cubes, enabling
users to extract and analyze the desired segments of data.

Parameters
----------
sector : int
    The TESS observation sector number.
camera : int
    The camera number (1-4).
ccd : int
    The CCD number (1-4).
